interactions.summary: Jiang defines monotheism as a radical novelty: once the Godhead formula is memorized, reality must be understood as God being nowhere and everywhere, which makes symbols become reality.
He uses money as the clearest example: unlike gold or silver, modern paper money is valuable because the symbol itself is treated as reality.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Jiang lecture monologue with imagined classroom exchange

interactions.summary: He lists monotheism's three modernizing characteristics: symbols become reality, other realities are crowded out, and expansion must destroy criticism until the system becomes true.

speaker_notes.note: The back-and-forth with people from a thousand years ago is Jiang's hypothetical example, not an actual exchange.

claims.claim: Monotheism is a radical idea because it asserts there is only one God and no other gods, unlike earlier one-God religions that still interacted with other gods.
The Godhead forces an understanding of reality where God is nothing and everything, causing representation and reality to collapse into one another.
Capitalism, science, and the nation state arise as modern ideas because monotheism trains people to treat symbols as reality.
Money is Jiang's best example of a symbol becoming reality: modern people value paper money because they believe money is nothing and everything.
Monotheism destroys alternative realities, especially spiritual realities, and must expand aggressively until its reality becomes self-fulfilling.
Science inherits monotheism's material-only reality and therefore refuses, in Jiang's view, to engage consciousness as a question central to being human.

signature_moments.moment: God is nowhere and everywhere; once that formula is accepted, symbols stop pointing at reality and become reality.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the episode's hinge from theology to modern institutions.

signature_moments.moment: Money is God because paper that can be printed without limit still commands reality.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It makes the abstract Godhead argument concrete through everyday capitalism.

signature_moments.moment: Monotheism becomes true by crowding out the possibility of asking whether the symbol has no value.
signature_moments.source_phrase: not allowed to imagine the possibility
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It captures Jiang's model of reality-maintenance through forbidden questions.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's formula for representation being conflated with reality, exemplified by money becoming wealth rather than representing wealth.
The requirement that monotheistic systems expand and destroy criticism so their reality becomes true.
The single reality left after monotheism crowds out spiritual realities; science is presented as operating only within it.

chronology_notes.note: This packet explicitly identifies capitalism, science, and the nation state as products of monotheism in the lecture's 2024-12-28 model.

uncertainty_notes.note: The sentence 'If consciousness is...' appears ASR-damaged; the surrounding point is that inability to explain consciousness blocks understanding humanity.
